THE SITUATION IN THE UNITED STATES (Source: "MORBIDITY AND MORTALITY-WEEKLY REPORT", U.S. National Office of Vital Statistics): Number of reported cases for the current week (previous week in parentheses):

During the first 15 weeks of this year of a total 290 cases of typhoid fever have been reported compared with 383 for the same period of 1956. This represents a 24 per cent reduction in the incidence from the previous year when several states expereinced an unusual number of cases. Although many of the states have shown a decrease in incidence, 3 North Central States (Michigan, Minnesota and Wisonsin) have reported a marked reduction - from a total of 56 cases last year to only 9 for the current yeaar. Several scattered states have reported increases.
